title: daphne gnidium subsp. gnidium
intro: Daphne gnidium subsp. gnidium, also known as the Gnidium spiritweed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Thymelaeaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Gnidium spiritweed include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its antimicrobial properties that can aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in traditional Greek medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues.
